title: Five Takeaways From the Birthright Citizenship Argument
summary: The justices grappled with questions about domiciles and foundlings, avoided policy debates and mused about the sweep of possible rulings.

title: Trump Attends Supreme Court Oral Arguments in a Presidential First
summary: President Trump’s presence in the court puts him face to face with justices whom he has tried to bully and intimidate.

title: Ending Birthright Citizenship Would Disproportionately Affect Asian Legal Immigrants
summary: A new study found that an end to universal birthright citizenship would disproportionately affect babies born to Asian parents.

title: Trump Says Europe Is On Its Own in Securing the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Threatening to pull out of NATO, President Trump portrayed the alliance as a “paper tiger” and said Europe was on its own in trying to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Placing U.S. Troops in Middle East Hotels May Violate Laws of War
summary: U.S. commanders have kept many troops away from bases in the region to protect them from Iran’s ballistic missile attacks.

title: Iraqi Kidnappers of Journalist Shelly Kittleson Offer to Negotiate
summary: The Iranian-aligned militia, Kataib Hezbollah, is pressing for the release of members detained by the Iraqi government in exchange for freeing the journalist, Shelly Kittleson.

title: C.D.C. Pauses Testing for Rabies and Mpox
summary: The diseases were removed from a list of tests the agency conducts for state and local health departments. Experts worry that with drastic staff reductions, the testing may not resume.

title: Homeland Security Dept. Asks Workers for Videos of Their Shutdown Hardships
summary: The department’s public affairs office is seeking 30-second “selfie videos,” part of a push to pressure lawmakers to strike a deal.

title: How NASA Is Keeping the Astronauts Safe During the Artemis II Mission
summary: Years of testing, emergency systems and sensors have gone into protecting the astronauts set to head to the moon.

title: How to Watch NASA’s Artemis II Moon Launch Online
summary: NASA will broadcast the launch of the Artemis II mission as well as holding live conversations with the crew during their time in space.

title: Who Murdered Minerliz? How a Diner Meet-up Cracked the Case.
summary: In 1999, a 13-year-old girl was found dead in the Bronx. Then the trail went cold for 20 years, until an ingenious ploy by detectives produced a vital clue to her killer.

title: Ocasio-Cortez Says She Will Oppose All U.S. Military Aid to Israel
summary: Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York said she would oppose U.S. military aid to Israel, including for defensive systems.

title: Democrats Start to Hammer Vulnerable Republicans Over War in Iran in Ads
summary: “Look at that gas pump,” a new ad from a liberal group says. It is targeting Representative Derrick Van Orden of Wisconsin over his support for the war effort.

title: South Dakotans React to Daily Mail Article on Bryon Noem, Kristi Noem’s Husband
summary: In the tiny town of Castlewood, S.D., where everyone knows the Noems, the prevailing sense was that people can’t help but feel bad for Bryon Noem after a tabloid photo leak.

title: One of Apple’s First Employees Looks Back at 50 Years
summary: In 1976, 14-year-old Chris Espinosa rode a moped to his job demonstrating computers made in Steve Jobs’s childhood home. The company has changed, but he’s still there.

title: Bruce Springsteen Brings Fiery Speeches and Songs to Minneapolis
summary: The E Street Band opened its Land of Hope and Dreams tour on Tuesday night, where the musician asked the crowd to choose “unity over division and peace over war.”

title: Tech Bros Hacked Their Diets. Now You May Be Doing It, Too.
summary: With seeds, supplements and gadgets (but little expert guidance), Americans of all stripes are seeking wellness through what they eat.

title: The Birthright Con
summary: Trump wants to find out just how weak this Supreme Court is.

title: Padma Lakshmi: The Decision That Would Create a Permanent American Underclass
summary: Trump wants to take away a fundamental American promise.

title: Born American
summary: We look at an important case before the Supreme Court.

title: F.D.A. Approves New Eli Lilly Obesity Pill, Foundayo
summary: The drug, made by Eli Lilly, could offer a more convenient option for patients who want to avoid injections.

title: Megan Thee Stallion Rushed to the Hospital During Moulin Rouge Broadway Performance
summary: The rapper was rushed to the hospital midway through a performance of “Moulin Rouge! The Musical” on Tuesday. She is expected to return to the show on Thursday.

title: A ‘Traffic Jam’ of Trials Poses Headache for Mangione’s Lawyers
summary: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers could be in and out of court in Manhattan through the end of the year. A judge could decide as early as Wednesday on whether to delay his federal case.

title: When Racism Is a Crime: Brazil Puts a Tourist on Trial for Word and Gesture
summary: An Argentine woman, who is white, could face years in prison after being accused of racism. The case has set off intense debate in Argentina and Brazil.
